description_long: This object is a large, pole-mounted solar panel array designed for urban energy generation. The array consists of multiple rectangular photovoltaic panels arranged in a grid, supported by a robust metal frame. The panels are mounted on a single vertical pole anchored to a concrete base, elevating the array above ground level. The surface of the panels is covered with a grid of blue-tinted solar cells, each separated by thin metallic lines. The mounting structure appears to be made of steel or aluminum, providing stability and durability. The overall design is utilitarian, optimized for maximum sunlight exposure and efficient energy capture in public or commercial outdoor spaces.
description: A pole-mounted solar panel array with a grid of blue photovoltaic cells, supported by a sturdy metal frame and concrete base. Designed for efficient solar energy capture in urban environments.
description_view_0: View from behind the solar panel array, showing the underside of the panels, support beams, and the central pole anchored to a square base.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the tilt of the solar panels and the structural frame supporting them.
description_view_2: Frontal view displaying the full grid of blue photovoltaic panels and the central supporting pole.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, similar to view 1, showing the panel tilt and frame from the other side.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, facing forward, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: pole-mounted, supported by a steel or aluminum frame with a concrete base
attribute_array_configuration: 4x5 grid of panels arranged in a rectangular array
